detaileddescription.para: Common rendering parameters for meshes and UI. Generated from class MR::BaseRenderParams. Derived classes: Direct: (non-virtual) MR::ModelBaseRenderParams MR::UiRenderParams Indirect: (non-virtual) MR::ModelRenderParams Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

Common rendering parameters for meshes (both for primary rendering and for the picker; the picker uses this as is while the primary rendering adds more fields). Generated from class MR::ModelBaseRenderParams. Base classes: Direct: (non-virtual) MR::BaseRenderParams Derived classes: Direct: (non-virtual) MR::ModelRenderParams Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

Mesh rendering parameters for primary rendering (as opposed to the picker). Generated from class MR::ModelRenderParams. Base classes: Direct: (non-virtual) MR::ModelBaseRenderParams Indirect: (non-virtual) MR::BaseRenderParams Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

Generated from class MR::BasicUiRenderTask::BackwardPassParams. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).
IRenderObject::renderUi() can emit zero or more or more of those tasks. They are sorted by depth every frame. Generated from class MR::BasicUiRenderTask.

Generated from class MR::UiRenderParams. Base classes: Direct: (non-virtual) MR::BaseRenderParams Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).
Generated from class MR::UiRenderManager. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

Generated from constructor MR::BaseRenderParams::BaseRenderParams. Parameter _other can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_BaseRenderParams_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_Matrix4f *viewMatrix, const MR_Matrix4f *projMatrix, MR_ViewportId viewportId, MR_Vector4i viewport)
memberdef.name: MR_BaseRenderParams_ConstructFrom

detaileddescription.para: Constructs MR::BaseRenderParams elementwise. Parameter viewMatrix can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to the parameter viewMatrix might be preserved in the constructed object. Parameter projMatrix can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to the parameter projMatrix might be preserved in the constructed object. The reference to the parameter viewportId might be preserved in the constructed object. The reference to the parameter viewport might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_BaseRenderParams_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it had previously.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Downcasts an instance of MR::BaseRenderParams to a derived class MR::ModelBaseRenderParams. This is a static downcast, it trusts the programmer that the target type is correct. Results in UB and returns an invalid pointer otherwise. This version is acting on mutable pointers.

Downcasts an instance of MR::BaseRenderParams to a derived class MR::ModelBaseRenderParams. This is a static downcast, it trusts the programmer that the target type is correct. Results in UB and returns an invalid pointer otherwise.

Downcasts an instance of MR::BaseRenderParams to a derived class MR::ModelRenderParams. This is a static downcast, it trusts the programmer that the target type is correct. Results in UB and returns an invalid pointer otherwise. This version is acting on mutable pointers.

Downcasts an instance of MR::BaseRenderParams to a derived class MR::ModelRenderParams. This is a static downcast, it trusts the programmer that the target type is correct. Results in UB and returns an invalid pointer otherwise.

Downcasts an instance of MR::BaseRenderParams to a derived class MR::UiRenderParams. This is a static downcast, it trusts the programmer that the target type is correct. Results in UB and returns an invalid pointer otherwise. This version is acting on mutable pointers.

Downcasts an instance of MR::BaseRenderParams to a derived class MR::UiRenderParams. This is a static downcast, it trusts the programmer that the target type is correct. Results in UB and returns an invalid pointer otherwise.

detaileddescription.para: The tasks are sorted by this depth, descending (larger depth = further away). Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::BasicUiRenderTask named renderTaskDepth. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.

The tasks are sorted by this depth, descending (larger depth = further away). Modifies a member variable of class MR::BasicUiRenderTask named renderTaskDepth. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. When this function is called, this object will drop object references it had previously in renderTaskDepth.

The tasks are sorted by this depth, descending (larger depth = further away). Returns a mutable pointer to a member variable of class MR::BasicUiRenderTask named renderTaskDepth. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.

briefdescription.para: Destroys a heap-allocated instance of MR_BasicUiRenderTask. Does nothing if the pointer is null.
Destroys a heap-allocated array of MR_BasicUiRenderTask. Does nothing if the pointer is null.

detaileddescription.para: This is an optional early pass, where you can claim exclusive control over the mouse. This pass is executed in reverse draw order. Generated from method MR::BasicUiRenderTask::earlyBackwardPass. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter params can not be null. It is a single object.
This is the main rendering pass. Generated from method MR::BasicUiRenderTask::renderPass. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Constructs an array of empty (default-constructed) instances, of the specified size. Will never return null. The array must be destroyed using MR_BasicUiRenderTask_BackwardPassParams_DestroyArray(). Use MR_BasicUiRenderTask_BackwardPassParams_OffsetMutablePtr() and MR_BasicUiRenderTask_BackwardPassParams_OffsetPtr() to access the array elements.

Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_BasicUiRenderTask_BackwardPassParams_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.
Generated from method MR::UiRenderManager::finishBackwardPass. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter params can not be null. It is a single object.

briefdescription.para: Destroys a heap-allocated instance of MR_IRenderObject. Does nothing if the pointer is null.
Destroys a heap-allocated array of MR_IRenderObject. Does nothing if the pointer is null.

detaileddescription.para: Generated from method MR::IRenderObject::render. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter params can not be null. It is a single object.
Generated from method MR::IRenderObject::renderPicker. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter params can not be null. It is a single object.
returns the amount of memory this object occupies on heap Generated from method MR::IRenderObject::heapBytes. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
returns the amount of memory this object allocated in OpenGL Generated from method MR::IRenderObject::glBytes. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.

binds all data for this render object, not to bind ever again (until object becomes dirty) Generated from method MR::IRenderObject::forceBindAll. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.

Render the UI. This is repeated for each viewport. Here you can either render immediately, or insert a task into params.tasks, which get Z-sorted.
params will remain alive as long as the tasks are used.
You'll have at most one living task at a time, so you can write a non-owning pointer to an internal task. Generated from method MR::IRenderObject::renderUi. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter params can not be null. It is a single object.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.
